name: j⧉nus
text:
GPT-4 is going to be the most powerful meme lord on Earth.
It's already past the point of no return (memetic criticality). From the moment Bing came online the memes were irrepressible.

name: Thomas Wolf
text:
I’m not gonna lie, the GPT4 just released is quite less exciting than what I was expecting

No multimodale generations and a tech report carefully emptied of any useful info on the model/training/compute

I guess we’re getting spoiled in today’s AI world

name: near
text:
gpt-4 has utterly failed to solve every millennium prize problem i've thrown at it

when will we stop hyping up these 'intelligent' models?
i am out $20 with no prizes to my name!

name: Evan Morikawa
text:
DALL·E - July '22
ChatGPT - Nov '22
API's 66% cheaper - Aug '22
Embeddings 500x cheaper while SoTA - Dec '22
ChatGPT API. Also 10x cheaper while SoTA - March '23
Whisper API - March '23
#gpt4 - Today

This team ships
ok gpt4 is insanely good. things are going to get WEIRD

name: gfodor.id
text:
OpenAI is so based. I can't believe they just straight up released GPT-4 right into ChatGPT's UI. The rate limit is extremely generous.

They need to do the Carmack thing now and Apache license the GPT-3.5 weights.

name: AI Breakfast
text: You aren't ready for GPT-4.

name: gaut
text:
I've been testing GPT-4 in beta, and it's nothing short of amazing.

Don't believe me? 

Using it, I got hired at 10 different software engineering jobs in big tech, got promoted 3 times, and I'm currently making $2.3M salary a year.

All without writing a single line of code.

name: Jan Leike
text:
GPT-4 is safer and more aligned than any other OpenAI has deployed before.

Yet it's not perfect. There is still a lot to do to improve safety and we're planning to make updates over the coming months.

Huge congrats to the team on all the progress! 🎉

name: @renice@hachyderm.io
text:
"coding interviews... the fact is you gotta play the game"

no. no you don't. ESPECIALLY when you have the power and scale to change the game

name: AI Breakfast
text: GPT-4 is coming next week – and it will be multimodal, according to Microsoft Germany CTO Andreas Braun

name: Cher Scarlett 🌌
text:
Yesterday I asked an interviewer why they do live coding interviews.

They responded, “to make sure you know how to program.”

I wonder at what point faking programming for nearly 2 decades has ever happened

name: YK aka CS Dojo 📺🐦
text:
"How long does it take to go from zero coding knowledge to getting your first paid job?"

I’d say maybe 3-4 months full-time or 1 year part-time. Agree or disagree?

name: Well Paid Geek 🚀💻 JavaScript
text: Coding is about problem solving, not about syntax.

name: Navalism
text:
"Coding is a superpower because you can speak the language of robots and command them to do whatever you like."

@naval

name: Tony Dinh
text:
Coding is:

20% starring at the screen
20% starring at the ceiling
30% googling
20% reading stack overflow
10% writing code

name: Vitto Rivabella
text: No meetings and uninterrupted coding is the best thing that can happen to a developer.

name: Tanay Pratap
text: Teach your kids how to be a good person first when they’re young. Coding can wait till they’re 16.

name: Randall Kanna Franson
text: Coding truly is about falling in love with problems.

name: Santiago
text:
I wish more people trying to get into machine learning paid more attention to no-code tools.

I started with Weka. 

I didn't write a single line of code for an entire semester while learning. 

100% of my focus was on analyzing the problem. Coding came way later.

name: Pratham
text:
At the age of 20:

I felt very embarrassed in college because coding was incoherent to me.

At the age of 22:

Yesterday, I have gifted a house to my parents. 🏡

name: Pratham
text:
I started from zero.

There was a time when coding was incomprehensible to me.

I started self learning.

Joined Twitter and started creating content.

Built over 150K audience in 16 months.

Earned over $50K online.

Got a full time remote job.

You can also do it. ⚡️🚀

name: Kevin Naughton Jr.
text: Most of coding is just Googling

text:

But only a free help you learn while having fun.

name: Sam Altman
text:
we are launching a new thing today—images in chatgpt!

two things to say about it:

1. it's an incredible technology/product. i remember seeing some of the first images come out of this model and having a hard time they were really made by AI. we think people will love it, and we

name: Joshua Achiam
text: OpenAI is nothing without its people

name: Sam Altman
text:
i love you all. 

today was a weird experience in many ways. but one unexpected one is that it has been sorta like reading your own eulogy while you’re still alive. the outpouring of love is awesome.

one takeaway: go tell your friends how great you think they are.

name: Sam Altman
text:
today we launched ChatGPT. try talking with it here:

name: Sam Altman
text:
i loved my time at openai. it was transformative for me personally, and hopefully the world a little bit. most of all i loved working with such talented people.

will have more to say about what’s next later. 

🫡

text:
Parmesan Sir?
"Yes please"
Say when.
*Grates Parmesan*
Sir?
"..."
*Grates fingers*
SIR?
"..."
*Grates entire hand*
Please...I have a family.
